series.name: Little Lanterns Lane
series.description: A gentle picture-book series for ages 6–8. Each story follows a child and a special night-lantern as they practice a small feeling-skill—with warmth, wonder, and caregiver-friendly read-aloud pacing.

titles.title: The Softest Glow
titles.subtitle: A Little Lanterns Lane Story of Quiet Courage
titles.blurb: Mira’s new night-lantern won’t blaze like the others—only a shy circle of light. She tries to force it brighter until the glow goes out. With Grandma’s help, she names what is true: homesick, mad, curious, brave-enough. The soft glow returns, and when neighbors visit, quiet light helps everyone settle.
titles.positioning: A warm bedtime picture book about quiet belonging—where a shy night-lantern teaches that soft light is still brave light.
insideBeats: A new room, a tea-colored lantern, and a glow that feels too shy.
Trying to force the light brighter—until it flickers out.
Naming what’s true: homesick, mad, curious, brave-enough.
Soft light returns, and helps the whole street settle for sleep.

caregiverNote.prompt: Tonight we can… Name one soft feeling before lights out.

lookInside.alt: Interior spread: neighboring lanterns blaze while Mira’s stays soft
Interior spread: the shy glow flickers out
Interior spread: quiet light helps everyone settle
Interior coda: Soft is still light—and it stayed

comingNext.label: Coming next
comingNext.note: More Little Lanterns Lane stories are on the way. No fake titles or ISBNs here—just a quiet placeholder for Book 2.
